questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: When 1 gram of oleum is diluted with water to make 100 mL solution. 100 mL of solution needs 100 mL of 0.24 M NaOH for complete neutralization. Select the correct statement(s) (Density of all solutions is $1 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{cc}$ )
options: Molarity of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ solution after dilution is 0.12 M
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& 100 \times \mathrm{M} \times \mathbf{2}=100 \times 0.24 \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4}=0.12 \mathrm{M} \\
& 2 \times \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4}=100 \times 0.24 \times 10^{-3} \\
& \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4}=0.012 \Rightarrow w_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4}=1.176 \mathrm{gm}
\end{aligned}$

So 1 gm oleum gives $1.176 \mathrm{gm} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ by consuming $\mathbf{0 . 1 7 6} \mathbf{g m}$ of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
$\%$ free $\mathrm{SO}_3$ in oleum = 78.2\%
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Which of the following solution(s) has/have higher moles of $\mathrm{H}^{+}$compared to 100 mL of 1 M HCl solution?
options: $\mathrm{5 0}$ gram aqueous solution with 0.2 mole fraction HCl
questions.solution: 1 mole HCl in 1000 g solvent 36.5 gm HCl in 1036.5 g solution 0.096 mole HCl in 100 g solution

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q:
Which of the following compound(s) has/have same number of atoms?
(i) $2 \mathrm{~mole}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$
(ii) 1 mole $\left.\mathrm{Fe}_{2( } \mathrm{SO}_4\right)_3$
(iii) 2 mole $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4$
(iv) 44.8 L of $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_6$ at STP
questions.solution: 2 mole $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ has 14 mole of atoms $1 \mathrm{~mole} \mathrm{Fe}_2\left(\mathrm{SO}_4\right)_3$ has 17 mole of atoms 2 mole $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4$ has 16 mole of atoms 44.8 L of $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_6$ has 16 mole of atoms
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
options: 4 normal concentration when reacted as given in the reaction $\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4+\mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{HPO}_4+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
3 molal concentration, if density of solution is $1.2 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mL}$
questions.solution:
2 molar $\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4$ has $2 \times 98 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4$ and $804 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ in 1000 g solution

Mole fraction of $\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4=0.043$
